_meta.description: Foreign sovereign-wealth, foreign-government, and non-campaign-finance money flows involving US public officials. Distinct from FEC contributions; sourced from primary government records and major investigative reporting.

fields.category: One of: foreign_government, sovereign_wealth, crypto, business_holdings, gift
fields.title: One-line headline
fields.amount: Dollar amount or range (string, e.g. '$57,355,532' or '~$5 billion')
fields.source: Source organization name (e.g. 'CREW', 'Office of Government Ethics')

Donald Trump.title: World Liberty Financial income (2024)
Donald Trump.summary: Income reported on Trump's June 2025 Office of Government Ethics financial disclosure from his ownership stake in World Liberty Financial, the Trump-family crypto venture launched in September 2024.
Donald Trump.source: Office of Government Ethics (2025 OGE Form 278)

Donald Trump.title: WLFI token launch windfall
Donald Trump.summary: Trump family members hold 22.5 billion $WLFI tokens. At the token's tradable launch in September 2025, the family stake was valued at approximately $5 billion. Tokens belonging to the founding Team remain locked pending a vesting schedule.

Donald Trump.title: WLFI revenue share to Trump family
Donald Trump.summary: Per WLFI's own published terms, 75% of the platform's net revenue flows to the Trump family. By March 2026, Forbes estimated Trump had netted ~$550 million from WLFI token sales; his WLFI stake was valued at $240M and his $WLFI tokens at $175M. Don Jr., Eric, and Barron each held stakes worth at least $133M.
Donald Trump.source: Reuters / CNBC / Forbes via Public Citizen

Donald Trump.title: First-term foreign government payments
Donald Trump.summary: CREW analysis documented $13.6M in payments from foreign governments to Trump businesses during his first term, building on House Oversight Committee findings that documented $7.8M from the more limited Mazars records. CREW calls $13.6M a conservative estimate; not included are trademarks awarded by China, Brazil, and the UAE.
Donald Trump.source: Citizens for Responsibility and Ethics in Washington (CREW)

Donald Trump.title: UAE 'Spy Sheikh' secret stake in Trump crypto
Donald Trump.entity: Sheikh Tahnoon bin Zayed Al Nahyan (UAE)
Donald Trump.summary: The Wall Street Journal reported that Sheikh Tahnoon bin Zayed, the UAE national security adviser known as the 'Spy Sheikh,' bought a secret stake in a Trump-affiliated crypto company.

Donald Trump.title: Trump Media & Technology Group (DJT)
Donald Trump.summary: Trump holds approximately 114 million shares of Trump Media & Technology Group through a trust. He benefits when share price increases, including from foreign government investment, advertising purchases, or official use of Truth Social to drive user traffic.

Donald Trump.title: Saudi PIF / LIV Golf relationship
Donald Trump.summary: Saudi Arabia's sovereign wealth fund finances LIV Golf, which has held multiple tournaments at Trump-owned properties. The Trump Organization receives hosting fees; the broader Saudi-Trump business relationship spans real estate (Dar Global tower projects in Dubai and Jeddah) and golf properties.

Donald Trump.title: Qatar 747 aircraft
Donald Trump.summary: Qatar offered a luxury Boeing 747-8 valued at approximately $400 million for use as Air Force One during Trump's second term. The arrangement drew immediate Emoluments Clause scrutiny.
